title: Neoporteria villicumensis
taxonomy: Neoporteria villicumensis is classified as a species of cactus in the genus Neoporteria. The genus Neoporteria is part of the family Echinocereus, which comprises a group of cacti commonly known as hedgehog cacti. The family Echinocereus is further classified within the subfamily Cactoideae, which is a large and diverse group of cacti. The subfamily Cactoideae is part of the family Cactaceae, which includes all species of cacti and their relatives. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics of Neoporteria villicumensis with other cacti.

intro: Neoporteria villicumensis, also known as the Villichumena cactus, is a species of cactus within the Eriosyce genus, native to the coastal regions of Chile. The Villichumena cactus has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Neoporteria villicumensis include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, often used to soothe respiratory issues and boost the immune system.
